steps.title: Compute total area to paint
steps.content: The wall area is $w$ square feet. Painting it twice means the total area covered by paint is $2w$ square feet.

steps.title: Divide by coverage per gallon
steps.content: Each gallon covers $260$ square feet, so the gallons needed are $$P = \frac{2w}{260} = \frac{w}{130}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $P=\frac{w}{130}$. Matches $\frac{2w}{260}$ exactly. Correct.
  • B: $P=520w$. Multiplies instead of divides — for $w=260$, this gives $135{,}200$ gallons, absurd.
  • C: $P=\frac{w}{260}$. This is gallons for one coat, not two.
  • D: $P=260w$. Multiplies instead of divides — nonsensical units.
That confirms A is correct.
